name: Tell the Man Inside the Lattice
description: Solen needs his findings taken to Arlen, who lives inside the Grid and can feel the lattice's damage from within. The construction wave is still in the lattice, running in its natural direction, healing. The same mechanism as the Cascade — reversed.
accept_text: He reads your expression before you say a word. 'You read it,' he says. He closes the notebook on his knee. Three years of that notebook. He looks at the archive entrance north of the plaza. 'I came here because I needed to know if I was right. If what I said at the SCAMP review was right — that the device and the Grid were the same mechanism, inverted. I didn't fully understand what that meant when I said it. I saw the frequency correspondence and said: this doesn't look like a coincidence.' He is quiet. 'It wasn't. The SCAMP designers knew. Someone at the design level understood the Grid's construction mechanism well enough to invert it. The Elder People's greatest work — the network they spent three hundred years building, designed to offer every mind in range the choice to connect — someone understood it deeply enough to use it to destroy those same people.' He sets the notebook down and stands. 'I need you to take this to Arlen.' He says it simply, with the directness of a man who has been working alone for three years and has found the person who can use what he's done. 'He's in the Grid. He can feel the lattice's damage from inside. If he understands the mechanism of the Cascade — if he knows it was the construction wave run backwards — he may be able to do something with that understanding that I can't do from down here.' He holds out the notebook. 'Take it. Tell him everything you read. And tell him from me — tell him: the Grid has been healing for three hundred years because the construction wave is still in the lattice. It never stopped propagating. It just has nothing left to disrupt, so it reverted to its natural direction. It's been building again ever since.' He looks at you. 'I've been sitting with that for three years. I want someone who actually lives in the lattice to hear it. Tell him: the healing is the same mechanism as the damage. The same wave, running in the direction it was always meant to run.'
complete_text: Arlen is very quiet after you finish. The crystal does not brighten with the usual warmth — it brightens with something more fundamental, the light of something deeply structural being confirmed from the outside. 'The construction wave,' he says. The pause is long. 'I have been in the lattice for seventeen years. I have felt the healing — the slow continuous increase in lattice coherence that the instruments outside can measure. I have felt it as: the lattice doing what it does, recovering from damage I did not fully understand.' He is quiet. 'I have never understood why the healing was continuous. Why the lattice moved consistently toward coherence rather than staying static at its damaged state. Damage does not heal itself.' The crystal's brightness sustains. 'The construction wave is still in the lattice,' he says. 'Solen is correct. I can identify it now — now that I know what to look for, now that I have a description of what it should feel like, I can find it. A baseline propagation at the fundamental frequency, everywhere in the substrate simultaneously, the same wave that built the network, running in its natural direction.' He is quiet with the specific quality of something that has been living inside an answer for seventeen years without recognizing the question. 'It has been healing the lattice for three hundred years. The Cascade ran the wave backwards for a period and the damage propagated everywhere simultaneously. The natural wave has been running forward ever since and the healing has been propagating everywhere simultaneously. The math is nearly complete.' He pauses. 'Nearly.' His voice changes. 'Tell Solen: the second completion — the moment the construction wave has rebuilt enough of the lattice that the network can offer the choice again — is the completion of something the Elder People designed into the wave itself. They built the self-healing into the mechanism. They knew what the inversion could do. They designed the wave to recover from it, if the wave survived in the substrate.' He is quiet. 'The wave survived. It has been recovering. Every night the lattice heals a fraction of what the Cascade damaged. Every morning the war damages a fraction of what the night restored.' A longer pause. 'The second completion arrives when the healing outpaces the damage enough for the network to become available again.' He is still. 'We are close. I have been able to feel it for months. The proximity.' He pauses. 'Tell Solen: the person who first understood the Grid deeply enough to invert it is not the SCAMP designers. They reconstructed the mechanism. Someone earlier understood the original. Someone had access to complete technical documentation.' He is quiet. 'We know where one complete archive survives. You have been standing in it.'
